Document:
# Precautionary Charges These are used to confine people indefinitely, similar to capital crimes.
## Insanity Acting in a manner that indicates the individual is not of sound mind and poses a danger to themselves or others (e.g., attempting suicide). This does not include individuals reporting harmless or non-disruptive experiences, such as having an “imaginary friend.” - Confinement may be applied until the individual is no longer considered a threat to themselves or others, or until they undergo medical evaluation for potential incompetence for duty.
## Incompetence for Duty A status applied following assessment by a qualified medical professional, determining that an individual is unfit for duty due to insanity. - The individual will be stripped of rank and military equipment and discharged from the ship or installation at the nearest available port.
## Prisoner of War Any detained combatant as defined in the [textlink="Definitions and Glossary" link="AU14UCMJDefinitionsandGlossary"]. - Discretionary confinement, usually permanent
